claims.text: The article states that the team proposed Prefill-as-a-Service (PrFaaS), a cross-data-center large-model inference service architecture.
The article states that PrFaaS enables KV Cache transmission across data centers and completely decouples Prefill and Decode onto different heterogeneous clusters.

The article states that in a hybrid architecture combining linear attention and full attention, linear layers produce fixed-size recurrent states that do not grow with context length, while only full-attention layers generate length-dependent KV Cache.
The article states that at 32K context, MiMo-V2-Flash has KV throughput of only 4.66Gbps, which is 13 times lower than MiniMax-M2.5.
The article states that at 32K context, Qwen3.5-397B has KV throughput of 8.25Gbps versus 33.35Gbps for a comparable dense model, a 4x reduction.

The article states that in tests on an internal 1T-parameter hybrid-attention model, the PrFaaS-PD architecture increased service throughput by 54% compared with a homogeneous PD cluster of the same hardware scale.
The article states that in the same experiments, the PrFaaS-PD architecture reduced P90 TTFT by 64%.

The article states that the PrFaaS cluster's average egress bandwidth was only 13Gbps on a 100Gbps cross-cluster link, and that ordinary commercial Ethernet could stably support KV Cache transfer without RDMA in this setup.
